data.narrative:
Simera Sense is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $16.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers in the sector, with three same-stage, same-sector comparables directly applicable.

Two valuation methods triangulate meaningfully. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $16M raised proxy, yielding $31M. Peer-median anchoring draws on three Series B space and aerospace companies—Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—with a median valuation of $400M and a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales cannot be applied; the sector median P/S across the five public comps is 49.8x, but Simera Sense's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated range spans $26M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $215M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly Aalyria's outlier valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, customer concentration, and stage-specific capital efficiency relative to the peer median. Stage-progression yields the most conservative estimate given early funding history; peer positioning within the $274M–$1.3B range will depend on traction metrics and competitive positioning relative to named comparables.
